generation.text: Explain angular momentum. The angular momentum of a particle with respect to a point \(O\) is defined as \(\vec{L}=\vec{r} \times \vec{p}\) where \(\vec{r}\) is the position vector of the particle with respect to O and \(\\vec{p}}=\vec{p} \times \ \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \times \{p} \times \vec{p} \times \vec{p} \
